reasoning: The timeline to close this recall is approximately 68 days (from April 24, 2025 to July 1, 2025). FSIS must complete a rigorous 'effectiveness check' to verify that the recalling firm has notified all of its customers and that the product has been retrieved and/or destroyed. This is a Class I recall (the highest risk level, due to an undeclared allergen), which typically requires 100% checks of consignees or a very robust sampling plan. The volume of the recalled product is significant (over 64,000 pounds), with lot dates spanning over a year (up to September 2026). Given typical government regulatory speeds and the complexity of ensuring a widely distributed frozen product is pulled from commerce, 68 days is a very tight window for the status to officially change from Active to Closed on the FSIS website. Therefore, it is highly unlikely to be closed before July 1.
